strategy.mission: We exist to discover and innovate to save and improve lives.
strategy.positioning: At heart we're a creative company. We invent things. We come up with new ways to make human and animal lives healthier and happier. We work together to solve challenges with new thinking.

corporateNames.usageRules: Use 'Merck' alone. Use 'Merck' along with a Division, functional area or business unit. 'Merck & Co., Inc.' may be used. 'Merck Sharp & Dohme' may be used.

corporateNames.region: Outside of the U.S. and Canada
corporateNames.usageRules: Use 'MSD' alone. Use 'MSD' along with a Division. 'Merck & Co., Inc.' may be used as a firm or corporate name but only if it includes full location. 'Merck Sharp & Dohme' may be used except for Germany. Germany: 'MSD Sharp & Dohme GmbH'.

disclaimers: Branding with both Merck and MSD in any form is not permitted. For internal use only, you may use the icon without the wordmark to represent both.

logos.overview: Our logo system includes primary and secondary variants, with or without the anthem 'Inventing for life'. The icon and wordmark have fixed relationships.

variants.name: Primary logo
variants.usageContext: Default logo. Icon on the left, wordmark on the right.

variants.name: Secondary logo
variants.usageContext: Stacked and centered. For use in restricted spaces only where horizontal logo cannot be accommodated.

variants.name: Primary logo with anthem
variants.usageContext: Standard primary layout with 'INVENTING FOR LIFE' underneath wordmark.

variants.name: Secondary logo with anthem
variants.usageContext: Stacked and centered layout with anthem. For restricted spaces.

variants.name: Icon only
variants.usageContext: Externally, only as social media channel icons. Internally, can be used freely.

clearSpace.formula: Proportional to 50% of the icon size

colorways.name: Primary
colorways.description: Teal icon with dark gray wordmark and anthem

colorways.name: Secondary
colorways.description: Teal icon with white wordmark and anthem

colorways.name: Alternative
colorways.description: White icon with white wordmark and anthem

dosAndDonts.description: Don't swap out the words Inventing for Life with a program, department, or franchise
Don't change the anthem font
Don't change the opacity or tint of our logo
Don't stretch our logo
Don't outline our Icon
Don't recolor our logo
Don't use our logo in all teal
Don't crop the logo
Don't run the logo vertically
Don't use our logo in white and gray on teal
Don't use our icon as a graphic language

primary.name: Merck Teal
primary.usage: Core brand color. Most important elements on digital such as CTAs, highlights.

primary.name: White
primary.usage: Background color, creates a light, modern and clean design.

accent.name: Merck Lime
accent.usage: Used in advanced visualization, infographics and charts.

accent.name: Merck Lemon
accent.usage: Used in advanced visualization, infographics and charts.

accent.name: Merck Pastel Blue
accent.usage: Used in advanced visualization, infographics and charts.

accent.name: Merck Vista Blue
accent.usage: Used in advanced visualization, infographics and charts.

accent.name: Merck Rich Blue
accent.usage: Used in advanced visualization, infographics and charts.

technicalNotes.conversionRules: Our RGB values have been optically adjusted for best results on screen and to adhere to legibility standards. They will not match the Pantone Matching System. Please only reference the values shown here.

dosAndDonts.description: Don't use accent colors when primary colors aren't present. Always establish primary colors first.
Don't use secondary colors as backgrounds in top-level communications.
Don't use accent colors in large amounts.
Don't use accent colors to highlight text.
Don't use UI colors as primary colors.

typefaces.name: Invention
typefaces.usageDescription: Primary custom typeface for expressing ourselves in a bold and unique way. Light for large headlines. Regular mainly for body copy. Bold for body copy headings and very small text.

typefaces.name: Arial
typefaces.usageDescription: Default fallback typeface only when Invention is not available (e.g. handed off to a third-party). Arial Narrow should be avoided.

hierarchy.name: Expressive Heading 1
hierarchy.contextUsage: Large expressive headings to create impact in marketing materials.

hierarchy.name: Formal Heading 1
hierarchy.contextUsage: Brochures, reports or whitepapers to divide up sections of content.

dosAndDonts.description: Avoid using italics in headlines.
Don't color headlines in Merck Teal with Merck Blue intro text.
Don't right align text.
Don't compress different layers of hierarchy together, give them space.
Don't run body copy in long lines, it becomes hard to read.
Don't run body copy in narrow columns.
Don't set Merck Blue text on Merck Teal backgrounds.
Don't set text in our accent colors.

voice.overview: Our tone of voice helps us to sound human and projects our personality. It is flexible and can be infused into almost all communications, with the exception of medical writing.

traits.name: Inventive
traits.description: We invent things. We come up with new ways to make lives healthier and happier.

traits.name: Empathetic
traits.description: We exist to serve others. We are driven by care and compassion.

traits.name: Relentless
traits.description: We have the confidence to take on the world's biggest challenges. We never give up.

writingTips: Write the way you speak. Play the dialogue in your head, then write it down.
Write less, edit more. Think about what your reader needs to know.
Adjust to your readers. Tailor vocabulary to your reader.

rules: Include a visual with every post.
Short posts work best. Use Tiny URLs to shorten links.
Use hashtags to connect to larger discussions, but in moderation (one to three).
Stay professional. Avoid casual posts about inconsequential things.

examples.before: Will we ever find a cure to Alzheimer's?
examples.after: Why is Alzheimer's so hard to defeat?

examples.before: You completed all the assigned tasks today.
examples.after: Thanks for everything you did today.

examples.before: We need to move forward proactively.

artDirectionCorePrinciples: We use natural lighting, rather than flash.
We capture candid moments, never overtly posing or staging.
We favor a natural eye-level viewpoint.
We favor normal lenses, rather than ultra wide angles.
We frame straight, not at jaunty angles.
We use a shallow depth of field to make it a focal point.

tiers.name: Tier 1: For life
artDirectionPointers: People captured in their natural environments

tiers.name: Tier 2: Invention
artDirectionPointers: Scientists are the focus, not the labs
Two or more scientists together shows collaboration
Use lab equipment as a frame around the subject

tiers.name: Tier 3: Values & culture
artDirectionPointers: Subject should never look directly into camera
Show the environment as realistically as possible
Use lines on buildings to help image composition

diversityAndInclusion.overview: Diversity and inclusion must be evident in all we communicate.
bestPractices: If showing more than one person, account for diversity among the group
Utilize photos of actual Merck employees

croppingGuidance.subject: people
rules: Do not crop out details that offer essential context.
Try using the rule of thirds.
Crop at eye level if you can.

croppingGuidance.subject: objects
rules: Ensure the hero subject and focus point remain.
Use the rule of thirds to add a dynamic composition.

illustration.overview: Allows us to express our ideas in a creative and inventive way on a White, Merck Teal or Merck Blue background.

styles.usage: Top-level brand messaging. Abstract, metaphorical or conceptual.

dataVisualization.overview: Visualize our world of data in a colorful way that is unique to us.

rules: Maintain good contrast between the colors
We don't use tints of our colors
Run the sequence again if more than eight data points

designSystem.overview: Our cellular grid system represents the idea of cellular growth, connecting images and copy to tell stories that grow organically.

expressions.description: Top-level applications (brochure covers, banners, advertising). Imagery grows organically from the Merck Teal Square on a white background.
Full bleed imagery for maximum impact. Uses the Teal Container to hold copy over the image.
Within communications (inside pages of reports/presentations). Grid is used to lay out content.

brandDevice.name: Merck Teal Square
usageRules: Never use within internal communications (secondary level)
placementRules: Place it on the corner of the image
Place it on the corner of the teal container (secondary expression)
Avoid placing it on faces within photography
brandDevice.sizingFormula: Should match the width of your margin

digital.overview: A consistent, functional and scalable digital design system built on a 4px baseline grid.

social.overview: Social media content uses grids for a consistent look across platforms, adapting to square, vertical 16:9, and horizontal formats.

postTypes.usage: Full bleed imagery allows us to create simple, bold and emotive posts.
Used to create environments which immediately feel Merck branded. Full bleed.
Impactful, hero statements with type. Kept short. Use full bleed color format.

brandingScale.guidance: Do not over-brand our world. Start without a logo in social designs and only add it if needed.

profileBranding.avatarRules: The Merck icon should be used consistently across all platforms as the profile image. Use plenty of padding.
profileBranding.bannerRules: Use full bleed photography and illustration. Ensure no key content becomes obstructed by the profile image.

applicationExamples.description: Secondary expression using full bleed image and teal text container
Vertical roller banner using primary expression
Instagram grid showcasing full bleed photos, illustrations, and typography
Brochure spread utilizing secondary colors, data visualization and Invention type
Large format posters using primary expression and cellular grid
